title: ditassa oliva-estevae
intro: Ditassa oliva-estevae is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family, native to Brazil. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, as well as having antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Ditassa oliva-estevae can be prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of conditions.
